title: strychnos diaboli
intro: Strychnos diaboli, also known as the devil's bush or poison devil, is a plant species in the Strychnaceae family, native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Strychnos diaboli include its use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or powders for medicinal use, and its roots, leaves, and seeds are commonly used in traditional African medicine.
